name: Discord Qdrant Triage
description:
Final Architecture: Discord → Qdrant Memory → GitHub Triage

 ### What changed

 You moved from a stateless lookup bot to an AI triage memory:
 - Qdrant stores two collections: (1) every GitHub issue + PR, (2) unmatched Discord reports.
 - New Discord posts are embedded and semantically searched against both collections.
 - LLM confirms matches (not just keyword search).
 - Unmatched reports live for 30 days and cluster by similarity.
 - When a cluster hits ≥3 reports, the bot drafts an aggregated summary and pings humans for approval — it does not
    auto-create the GitHub issue.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### Qdrant Collections

 ┌───────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Collection        │ Payload Schema                                          │ Purpose                          │
 ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
 │ github_issues     │ number, state, title, body, url, is_pr, updated_at,     │ Semantic index of all issues +   │
 │                   │ embedding                                               │ open PRs. Refreshed on schedule. │
 ├───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
 │ discord_unmatched │ thread_id, author, summary, url, report_count,          │ Active memory of reports with no │
 │                   │ first_seen, last_seen, related_thread_ids[], embedding  │ GitHub match. Clusters grow      │
 │                   │                                                         │ here.                            │
 └───────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┘

 │ 30-day TTL: Use Qdrant payload filtering (last_seen > now - 30d) on every search. Add a nightly Kestra cleanup
 │ flow to delete dead points.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### Kestra Flows

 #### Flow 1: github_sync_daily

 Trigger: Every 6 hours
 What it does:
 1. Paginate GitHub REST/GraphQL for all issues (open+closed) and open PRs.
 2. For each, compute OpenAI text-embedding-3-small vector of title + "\n" + body.
 3. Upsert into Qdrant github_issues collection (issue_number as point ID to avoid duplicates).
 4. Mark deleted/closed issues for archival (keep in DB but tag archived=true).

 #### Flow 2: discord_triage (Main)

 Trigger: Webhook from Cloudflare Worker relay

 ┌──────────────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Step             │ Task             │ Details                                                                  │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 0. Gate          │ KV check         │ Skip if thread_id already processed.                                     │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 1. Fetch thread  │ Discord REST API │ Pull all messages in the forum thread.                                   │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 2. Summarize     │ OpenAI           │ Generate a concise bug summary (2–3 sentences) + severity guess +        │
 │                  │ minimax-m2.7      │ product area tags.                                                       │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 3. Embed         │ OpenAI           │ Compute embedding of the summary.                                        │
 │                  │ Embeddings API   │                                                                          │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 4. Search GitHub │ Qdrant search    │ Top 5 hits from github_issues collection, cosine similarity > 0.80.      │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 5. GitHub Match? │ LLM Judge        │ Prompt: "Given this Discord report and 5 candidate GitHub issues, is     │
 │                  │ (minimax-m2.7)    │ this the same bug? Return: match_found (bool), matched_issue_number (int │
 │                  │                  │ or null), confidence (0–1), reasoning."                                  │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 6a. MATCH        │ Parallel         │ Discord reply: "Tracked in #XXX" + GitHub comment with Discord link +    │
 │                  │                  │ inline attachments.                                                      │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 7b. NO MATCH →   │ Qdrant search    │ Top 5 from discord_unmatched, filtered last_seen > now - 30d, cosine     │
 │ Search Memory    │                  │ similarity > 0.82.                                                       │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 8b. Cluster      │ LLM Judge        │ Prompt: "Is this new report describing the same issue as any of these    │
 │ Match?           │                  │ past unmatched reports?"                                                 │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 9b.1 Existing    │ Update Qdrant    │ Increment report_count, append thread_id, update last_seen.              │
 │ cluster          │                  │                                                                          │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 9b.2 New cluster │ Insert Qdrant    │ Create new point with report_count=1.                                    │
 ├──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ 10b. Threshold   │ Conditional      │ If report_count >= 3: trigger Flow 3. Otherwise: Discord reply "This is  │
 │ check            │                  │ a new issue we are looking into."                                        │
 └──────────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 #### Flow 3: triage_draft_alert (Triggered by Flow 2)

 What it does:
 1. Fetch all related Discord thread IDs and summaries from Qdrant.
 2. Call OpenAI GPT-4o to write a draft GitHub issue body:
     - Title: AI-generated from common symptoms.
     - Body: Aggregated user reports, links to all Discord threads, environment hints, attachments log.
 3. Post to a designated Discord admin/triage channel:
     - "@maintainers — 3+ users reported a similar issue."
     - Draft title + body.
     - List of Discord thread links.
     - "React ✅ to auto-create this issue, or edit the draft below."
 4. Update Qdrant discord_unmatched point with alert_sent=true and draft_body.

 #### Flow 4: memory_cleanup

 Trigger: Daily at 03:00 UTC
 What it does:
 1. Delete Qdrant discord_unmatched points where last_seen < now - 30d.
 2. Log how many clusters were purged and what max report_count was before death.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### Attachment Handling (Revised)

 ┌───────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Type      │ Action                                                                                             │
 ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Images    │ Discord CDN URL embedded in markdown. Kestra downloads and mirrors to an S3/R2 bucket for          │
 │           │ permanence, linking the mirror URL in the GitHub comment.                                          │
 ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Logs /    │ Downloaded, truncated to 5,000 chars, pasted as <details><summary>Log</summary><pre> in markdown.  │
 │ text      │                                                                                                    │
 ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Videos    │ Same as images — mirrored to S3/R2, linked (GitHub comments don't embed video).                    │
 └───────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### Rate Limits & Costs (Medium volume: 20–200 posts/day)

 ┌────────────────────┬───────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Resource           │ Limit             │ Estimated usage                                          │
 ├────────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ GitHub Search/REST │ 5,000/hour        │ Sync 1×/6h + per-post lookups ≈ well within limits.      │
 ├────────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Discord REST       │ 50 req/s globally │ Each post = 1 thread fetch + 1 reply ≈ trivial.          │
 ├────────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ OpenAI Embeddings  │ ~10k RPM          │ Each post = 1 embedding + sync batch ≈ negligible.       │
 ├────────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ OpenAI GPT         │ TPM-dependent     │ Each post = 1 summarize + 1–2 judge calls ≈ ~$0.02/post. │
 ├────────────────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ Qdrant             │ Self-hosted       │ CPU-bound on query volume; 200 queries/day is nothing.   │
 └────────────────────┴───────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 │ Monthly AI cost estimate: ~$12–$120 depending on exact token counts.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### Cloudflare Worker Relay (Updated contract)

 Worker receives Discord THREAD_CREATE + MESSAGE_CREATE (for initial post) events, validates signature, and POSTs
 to Kestra webhook:

 ```json
   {
     "thread_id": "...",
     "channel_id": "...",
     "guild_id": "...",
     "author": {"id": "...", "username": "..."},
     "content": "raw message text",
     "attachments": [
       {"url": "https://cdn.discordapp.com/.../image.png", "filename": "image.png", "size": 12345}
     ],
     "timestamp": "2026-06-04T12:00:00Z",
     "message_id": "...",
     "initial_message_id": "..."
   }
 ```

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### What You Need Running Before Day 1

 1. Qdrant container (Docker) or Qdrant Cloud cluster.
 2. Kestra instance with:
     - KV store enabled.
     - HTTP/OpenAI tasks available.
     - Network access to Qdrant, Discord, GitHub.
 3. Cloudflare Worker deployed with Discord app credentials.
 4. S3/R2 bucket (optional but recommended) for mirrored attachments. You can skip for MVP and use Discord CDN URLs
    directly.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### The Two LLM Judge Prompts

 Prompt A: GitHub match

 ```
   You are a senior support engineer.
   A user reported this issue on Discord:
   ---
   {discord_summary}
   ---

   Here are up to 5 candidate GitHub issues:
   1. #{n1}: {title1}
      {body1}
   2. ...

   Is the Discord report describing the same root cause as any candidate?
   Respond ONLY in JSON:
   {"match_found": bool, "issue_number": int|null, "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "reasoning": "..."}
 ```

 Prompt B: Memory cluster match

 ```
   You are tracking duplicate bug reports.
   New report:
   ---
   {new_summary}
   ---

   Past unmatched reports (up to 5):
   1. {past_summary_1}
   2. ...

   Is the new report the same issue as any past report?
   Respond ONLY in JSON:
   {"same_cluster": bool, "matched_memory_id": string|null, "confidence": 0.0-1.0}
 ```

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ### Unresolved Detail

 When the bot hits ≥3 and drafts the issue summary:
 - Where does the human approve it? A Discord admin channel, a GitHub discussion thread, or a dedicated Kestra UI?
 - What triggers the actual GitHub issue creation? A Discord reaction button, a manual Kestra execution, or a
   GitHub action workflow?

 My recommendation: post the draft in a private Discord admin channel with a 🔀 reaction. When a maintainer reacts,
 a second Cloudflare Worker (or Kestra webhook from Discord interactions) receives the interaction and triggers a
 Kestra flow that creates the real GitHub issue, then moves the cluster data from discord_unmatched → a new
 pending_issues collection.

goal.answer: Implement the MVP architecture for a Discord → Qdrant Memory → GitHub Triage system. A single Cloudflare Worker relays Discord forum posts and interaction webhooks to Kestra Cloud flows. The flows embed reports, semantically match them against indexed GitHub issues via LLM judges, cluster unmatched reports in Qdrant, draft aggregated issues when clusters hit ≥3 reports, and route human approval through Discord reactions back to Kestra to create real GitHub issues.

criteria.answer:
Cloudflare Worker compiles and handles both relay (THREAD_CREATE) and interaction (reaction approval) webhooks
Four Kestra flow YAMLs are present and syntactically valid: github_sync_daily, discord_triage, triage_draft_alert, memory_cleanup
Qdrant collection setup script creates github_issues, discord_unmatched, and pending_issues collections with correct schemas
LLM judge Prompt A (GitHub match) and Prompt B (cluster match) are committed as standalone templates
README documents Day-1 setup: Discord app credentials, Kestra Cloud namespace, Qdrant Cloud connection, GitHub token, OpenAI key, and deployment steps
Worker smoke-test commands (curl relay + curl interaction) are documented and runnable

constraints.answer: MVP scope: skip S3/R2 attachment mirroring; use Discord CDN URLs directly, Kestra Cloud for orchestration — no Docker Compose or self-hosted Kestra, Qdrant Cloud for vector store — no self-hosted container infrastructure, One Cloudflare Worker handles both relay and Discord interaction webhooks via route differentiation, No frontend UI for human approval; approval happens entirely via Discord reactions

phases.answer: Worker & Foundation, Kestra Flows, Setup & Docs

assumptions.answer: Greenfield workspace with no existing code to migrate or refactor; Step 4 deep exploration was skipped because there was no existing codebase; GitHub authentication uses a Personal Access Token (simpler than GitHub App for MVP); OpenAI API key is available as a Kestra secret or environment variable; Discord bot has Read Messages, Send Messages, Add Reactions, and Read Message History permissions; Kestra Cloud namespace allows outbound HTTP to Qdrant Cloud, Discord REST, and GitHub REST; Collections are initialized before flows run (via setup script documented in README); User has Wrangler CLI configured for Worker deployment; The 30-day TTL for discord_unmatched is enforced via Qdrant payload filtering in search + nightly cleanup flow, not via automatic expiration; All collected data is stored in vectors; payload filtering uses last_seen timestamps for memory cleanup

phases.name: Worker & Foundation
phases.goal: Build and typecheck the Cloudflare Worker with Discord webhook routing, signature verification, and Kestra relay

steps.description: Scaffold repo structure: worker/, kestra/flows/, prompts/, scripts/; create wrangler.toml, package.json, tsconfig.json

verification.command: test -f worker/wrangler.toml && test -f worker/package.json && test -f worker/tsconfig.json

steps.description: Implement Discord ed25519 webhook signature verification using Web Crypto API and route requests to relay or interaction handlers
Implement THREAD_CREATE relay handler: validate signature, format payload, POST to Kestra webhook URL
Implement interaction handler for admin channel reactions: parse MESSAGE_REACTION_ADD, trigger Kestra flow for GitHub issue creation
Define shared TypeScript types for Discord webhook payloads, interaction payloads, and Kestra relay contracts
Install dependencies and verify worker TypeScript compiles with no errors

verification.command: cd worker && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete. Scaffolded the Cloudflare Worker project with pnpm, implemented Ed25519 signature verification via Web Crypto API, built routing for THREAD_CREATE relay (with Discord API enrichment), MESSAGE_COMPONENT interactions (approve/reject buttons), and MESSAGE_REACTION_ADD approval events. Consolidated all shared TypeScript types into types.ts. Verified zero TypeScript compilation errors across all worker source files. Dependencies are installed and reproducible.

phases.name: Kestra Flows
phases.goal: Write and validate all four Kestra flow YAML definitions for the orchestration pipeline

steps.description: Write github_sync_daily.yaml: schedule trigger every 6h, paginate GitHub issues/PRs, compute embeddings, upsert to Qdrant
Write memory_cleanup.yaml: daily 03:00 UTC trigger, delete Qdrant discord_unmatched points older than 30 days, log stats
Write discord_triage.yaml: webhook trigger, KV gate, fetch thread, summarize, embed, search GitHub, LLM judge, branch match/no-match, search memory, cluster judge, upsert, threshold check
Write triage_draft_alert.yaml: triggered by discord_triage threshold, fetch cluster from Qdrant, GPT-4o draft, Discord admin alert, update Qdrant with alert_sent=true
Validate all Kestra flow YAML files parse as valid YAML

verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml, sys; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in sys.argv[1:]]" kestra/flows/*.yaml

phases.summary: Phase 2 complete. Wrote four Kestra flow YAMLs in kestra/flows/: github_sync_daily (6h schedule, GitHub pagination → OpenAI embeddings → Qdrant upsert), memory_cleanup (daily 03:00 UTC, deletes discord_unmatched points older than 30 days), discord_triage (webhook-triggered, KV-gated flow with Python script that fetches Discord thread, summarizes, embeds, searches GitHub with LLM Judge A, searches memory with LLM Judge B, upserts, and threshold-checks), and triage_draft_alert (Switch-routed flow for drafting GPT-4o issues, Discord admin alerts with buttons, and GitHub issue creation on approval). All four YAMLs validated as syntactically parseable using Python yaml.safe_load.

phases.name: Setup & Docs
phases.goal: Document Day-1 setup, extract prompt templates, create Qdrant initialization script, and provide smoke tests

steps.description: Create Qdrant setup script to initialize github_issues, discord_unmatched, and pending_issues collections with correct vector dimensions (1536 for text-embedding-3-small)
Extract and commit LLM judge Prompt A (GitHub match) and Prompt B (cluster match) as standalone markdown templates in prompts/
Write comprehensive README with architecture overview, prerequisites, environment variables, Discord/Kestra/Qdrant setup, deployment steps, and operational notes

verification.command: test -s README.md && grep -q 'Discord' README.md && grep -q 'Qdrant' README.md && grep -q 'Kestra' README.md

steps.description: Add smoke test commands: curl example for relay webhook and curl example for Discord interaction approval webhook
Final review: verify all expected deliverable files exist and are committed in the repository

verification.command: test -f worker/src/index.ts && test -f kestra/flows/discord_triage.yaml && test -f kestra/flows/triage_draft_alert.yaml && test -f kestra/flows/github_sync_daily.yaml && test -f kestra/flows/memory_cleanup.yaml && test -f prompts/prompt_a_github_match.md && test -f prompts/prompt_b_cluster_match.md && test -f scripts/setup_qdrant.py && test -f README.md

phases.summary: Phase 3 complete. Created scripts/setup_qdrant.py to initialize three Qdrant collections (github_issues, discord_unmatched, pending_issues) with 1536-dim Cosine vectors and payload indexes. Extracted two LLM judge prompt templates into prompts/ as standalone markdown files. Wrote comprehensive README.md with architecture diagram, prerequisites, environment variables, setup instructions for Discord/Kestra/Qdrant/Worker, deployment steps, operational notes, and curl smoke-test examples. Added executable tests/smoke.sh. Committed all deliverables to git with a proper .gitignore.

goal: Implement the MVP architecture for a Discord → Qdrant Memory → GitHub Triage system. A single Cloudflare Worker relays Discord forum posts and interaction webhooks to Kestra Cloud flows. The flows embed reports, semantically match them against indexed GitHub issues via LLM judges, cluster unmatched reports in Qdrant, draft aggregated issues when clusters hit ≥3 reports, and route human approval through Discord reactions back to Kestra to create real GitHub issues.
successCriteria: Cloudflare Worker compiles and handles both relay (THREAD_CREATE) and interaction (reaction approval) webhooks
Four Kestra flow YAMLs are present and syntactically valid: github_sync_daily, discord_triage, triage_draft_alert, memory_cleanup
Qdrant collection setup script creates github_issues, discord_unmatched, and pending_issues collections with correct schemas
LLM judge Prompt A (GitHub match) and Prompt B (cluster match) are committed as standalone templates
README documents Day-1 setup: Discord app credentials, Kestra Cloud namespace, Qdrant Cloud connection, GitHub token, OpenAI key, and deployment steps
Worker smoke-test commands (curl relay + curl interaction) are documented and runnable
constraints: MVP scope: skip S3/R2 attachment mirroring; use Discord CDN URLs directly
Kestra Cloud for orchestration — no Docker Compose or self-hosted Kestra
Qdrant Cloud for vector store — no self-hosted container infrastructure
One Cloudflare Worker handles both relay and Discord interaction webhooks via route differentiation
No frontend UI for human approval; approval happens entirely via Discord reactions

grade.rationale: Phase 1 verified the Worker only with tsc --noEmit, Phase 2 validated Kestra YAMLs via yaml.safe_load, and Phase 3 checked setup_qdrant.py with ast.parse, none of which actually executed the artifacts in a runtime environment. C3 overstates these static checks as 'real verification,' and without an available diff to audit end-to-end implementation coherence, the work satisfies the goal but with weak execution-level substantiation.
